# Vike — documentation index for AI agents

Vike is a Vite-based framework for server-side rendering (SSR), static generation (SSG), and single-page apps (SPA). This index maps Vike's concepts to their docs so you fetch the right page before writing or changing Vike code.

- Read the whole file first; entries are ordered hardest-to-guess first.
- https://vike.dev is authoritative — for any non-trivial task fetch https://vike.dev + the path shown and follow it; don't rely on memory or on compiled output under node_modules/vike/dist.
- Values seen in source code, especially a hook's environment, are overridable defaults, not limits; the linked page states what's configurable.
- Each entry is "## Title - /path" followed by a dense list of use cases, core concepts, and keywords.

## meta - /meta
Define custom config and custom hooks; override built-in config behavior, e.g. relocate a hook's environment (make server-default +data isomorphic or client-only) via a config-time effect. Per-config env declaration (server, client, config-time, production-only), cumulative merge-vs-replace, eager loading across all pages, global-only configs, vite-reload trigger; extensibility foundation. Extend typings through the Vike.Config and Vike.ConfigResolved global interfaces.

## config - /config
The +file convention (+config, +Page, +route, +data...); config inheritance parent-dir → child-page giving global defaults plus per-group and per-page overrides; cumulative vs override settings; .clear suffix stops inheritance, .default suffix marks a fallback. Pointer imports so config loads without pulling runtime code; config files never enter client/server bundles. Config type, vike/types, extends.

## pageContext - /pageContext
Per-render object: parsed URL, route params, request data, navigation flags, custom properties; created fresh per server request and per client navigation, scoped to one render; the server→client boundary — only passToClient'd properties reach the browser. urlParsed, urlPathname, urlOriginal, routeParams, isHydration, isClientSideNavigation, isBackwardNavigation, previousPageContext, abortReason, abortStatusCode; PageContextServer / PageContextClient typings; read in components via usePageContext; outdated-pageContext-on-hydration caveat for prerendered pages.

## globalContext - /globalContext
App-wide state outliving any single render: global services, page inventory, production assets manifest, runtime config. Singleton — one per server process, one per browser tab — vs the per-render pageContext; lifetime = environment lifetime. Initialized in onCreateGlobalContext, reached anywhere via getGlobalContext or pageContext.globalContext; GlobalContextServer / GlobalContextClient, globalContext.pages, prerenderContext, viteConfig.

## file-env - /file-env
Filename suffixes enforcing or relocating execution environment; guarantee a module loads only one side; prevent secret leaks. .server (throws if bundled for browser), .client (browser-only), .shared (isomorphic), .ssr (initial server render only, not client navigation — store init). On +files these suffixes shift a hook's declared env, equivalent to meta.env; move +data/+guard/+onBeforeRender on/off the client.

## passToClient - /passToClient
Expose server-computed pageContext properties to the browser beyond built-in defaults; required for client-side access to server data; hydration data. Accumulating list of property paths, dot-notation for nested fields, serialized and shipped. Structured JSON serializer (@brillout/json-serializer) — plain data round-trips, custom class instances are lost.

## data - /data
Fetch a page's primary data before render; result on pageContext.data, read via useData; direct DB/ORM/SQL queries. Env defaults to server-side but is configurable to client-only or isomorphic via file suffixes or globally via meta; result serialized automatically. For prerendered (SSG) pages, client-side +data fetches fresh values at request time instead of freezing them at build; abort with throw render / throw redirect; pageContext.json requests.

## onBeforeRender - /onBeforeRender
Lower-level alternative to +data: set multiple arbitrary pageContext properties, integrate a lower-level tool, orchestrate meta-driven custom hooks. Runs before render; page-level definition replaces the global one (not cumulative); nothing reaches the client unless listed in passToClient (unlike +data which sends everything). Env server-by-default, relocatable; pairs with onBeforeRenderHtml / onBeforeRenderClient.

## useConfig - /useConfig
Set head / HTML-shell settings dynamically from fetched data — title, meta tags, og:image computed at request time. The one universal hook usable both inside UI components and inside Vike hooks; must be called before any await in async hooks. Declarative Config and Head components wrap it; provided by vike-react/vue/solid.

## hooks - /hooks
Index of every built-in hook with its environment and one-line role; execution order and lifecycle/flow diagram; how to choose a hook. Groups: basics, pre-rendering, instrumentation, advanced; distinguishes configurable-env hooks (data, guard, onBeforeRender) from fixed-env ones (onRenderHtml, onRenderClient, onBeforeRoute, onData); custom hooks created via meta.

## onCreatePageContext - /onCreatePageContext
Seed or compute pageContext properties immediately after routing, before any other page hook; per-request server-side store; extend pageContext with custom data. Runs once per page request, both sides by default, cumulative across page groups, scopable by file suffix (.ssr.js). Contrast with onCreateGlobalContext: per navigation, not once per process.

## onCreateGlobalContext - /onCreateGlobalContext
One-time setup tied to the long-lived singleton: fetch shared data, wire a client store, bootstrap session/auth state. Runs once when globalContext is built, both sides by default, global and cumulative, scopable by file suffix. Contrast with onCreatePageContext: once per process/session, not per navigation.

## onHookCall - /onHookCall
Wrap every other hook for instrumentation — OpenTelemetry tracing, Sentry spans, timing/logging, performance monitoring. Receives a handle exposing hook.name, hook.filePath, and hook.call() which must run before any await to preserve sync semantics. Global, cumulative, server-default-but-relocatable; beta.

## onData - /onData
Post-process the +data result after fetch, before components consume it, both sides; normalize or enrich values centrally. Accumulating transform layer over fetched page data; middleware-style step around the data hook output.

## onError - /onError
Report server-side exceptions to monitoring (Sentry, Datadog); observability, not render control. Server-only, global, cumulative; called on any server error with (error, pageContext) where pageContext may be null. Gate behind production check (import.meta.env.PROD).

## routing-precedence - /routing-precedence
Resolve conflicts when several routes match one URL; control priority. Specificity order: positive-precedence Route Functions → static Route Strings → Filesystem Routes → parameterized Route Strings → unranked Route Functions → negative-precedence Route Functions; fewer wildcards win among patterns. Numeric precedence returned from a Route Function is the manual override.

## route-function - /route-function
Programmatic matching needing logic — request/role-aware conditions, regex, third-party matchers, auth-gating at route level. Exported from +route, receives pageContext, returns false or { routeParams, precedence }; must be synchronous and lightweight since all run on every navigation; can call resolveRoute() internally; cannot inject pageContext values. partRegex, path-to-regexp, vike/routing.

## route-string - /route-string
URL pattern with named and wildcard segments — /product/@id, catch-all globs; the common parameterized-route case. Assigned to +route, surfaces matches as pageContext.routeParams, overrides Filesystem Routing; @param segments, * glob, resolveRoute. Switch to a Route Function when conditions/regex are needed.

## filesystem-routing - /filesystem-routing
How file paths map to URLs and which directory names are dropped; domain-driven file structure; renderer/ directory. Stripped segments: pages/, index/, src/, renderer/, parenthesized (group) dirs; Git-aware crawl skips gitignored files (VIKE_CRAWL); case sensitivity. Distinct from config inheritance's folder walk; overridden by Route Strings / Functions.

## route - /route
Override a page's filesystem-derived URL with an explicit definition. The +route value is a Route String pattern or a Route Function for full control; when present it fully replaces the location-derived URL. Delegates to the string or function mechanisms.

## routing - /routing
Overview of the three routing mechanisms — Filesystem Routing, Route Strings, Route Functions — plus organizing pages into parenthesized groups and gating access with +guard. Route parameters, +route.ts overrides. Entry point before the specific routing pages.

## guard - /guard
Authorize access before any data is fetched — blocks unauthorized data fetching at the earliest safe point; auth checks, role-based protection, redirect unauthenticated users. Returns nothing; aborts via throw render() or throw redirect(); runs after routing, before +data; env mirrors +data (server-default, relocatable); applies to one page or a subtree.

## render - /render
throw render() inside any hook: abort and show the error page with a status code (401/403/404/429/500/503) or render a different URL's page — the browser URL does not change (vs throw redirect). Passes abortReason and abortStatusCode to pageContext for the error page; URL rewrite without redirect; common in guard/data for access-denied and error handling. vike/abort.

## redirect - /redirect
throw redirect() inside any hook: abort and send the visitor to another URL, changing the browser address and HTTP response; default 302, pass 301 for permanent. Differs from throw render (URL preserved) and navigate() (runs after render); most common in guard/data for auth flows. vike/abort.

## error-page - /error-page
The single special error page rendered for 404 (no route match), 500 (unhandled hook error), or explicit throw render(statusCode); access-denied messages. Reads pageContext.is404, abortStatusCode, abortReason to tailor the message; emits 404.html in prerendered builds. Located at the _error page.

## clientRouting - /clientRouting
Switch from server routing (full page loads) to client routing (in-process navigation) with transitions and intercepted <a> clicks. Requires onRenderClient to handle both initial hydration (isHydration) and later navigations; unlocks onHydrationEnd, onPageTransitionStart/End, and navigate(); opt links out with data-vike="false" or rel="external". Global boolean defining the SPA-style experience.

## onHydrationEnd - /onHydrationEnd
Run setup once the first visited page becomes interactive — post-hydration init, analytics, third-party widgets needing live DOM. Browser-only; fires once after onRenderClient hydrates the first page; not called on later navigations (use onPageTransitionEnd). Requires Client Routing.

## onPageTransitionStart - /onPageTransitionStart
Drive navigation animations or toggle CSS classes as a client-side navigation begins, before the next page renders; detect backward navigation. Browser-only; paired with onPageTransitionEnd; receives a partial pageContext including isBackwardNavigation. Requires Client Routing.

## navigate - /navigate
Programmatic navigation from code — after a form submit, any link-less transition. Browser-only; routes through the Client Router, returns a promise resolving when the destination has rendered; options keepScrollPosition, overwriteLastHistoryEntry, pageContext passthrough. Differs from throw redirect (pre-render abort) and reload (same URL); vike/client/router; requires Client Routing.

## reload - /reload
Re-render the current URL in place — refresh after auth/cookie change, logout. Routes through the Client Router, faster than window.location.reload (no full browser reload); ≈ navigate(current URL) with edge cases handled. Browser-only; requires Client Routing.

## prefetch - /prefetch
Warm a page's assets ahead of an anticipated transition to cut perceived latency; imperative/programmatic, fire-and-forget. The code-driven complement to declarative hover/viewport asset loading; vike/client/router. Browser-only; requires Client Routing.

## keepScrollPosition - /keepScrollPosition
Don't jump to top on navigation — tabbed interfaces, nested layouts sharing a frame. Default scrolls to top; true preserves position across pages sharing route params; a string value forms a named scroll group spanning arbitrary pages; a function computes grouping per render. Browser-side, tied to Client Routing.

## prefetchStaticAssets - /prefetchStaticAssets
When linked pages' assets load — 'hover', 'viewport', or false. Declarative link prefetching, overridable per link via a data attribute; requires Client Routing; viewport mode disabled in dev. The declarative counterpart to calling prefetch() imperatively.

## preloading - /preloading
Control which <script>, stylesheet, and preload tags Vike injects and when; reduce round trips. injectFilter (returned from onRenderHtml) overrides inclusion and placement; pageContext.httpResponse.earlyHints for 103 Early Hints; production assets manifest via getGlobalContext.

## pre-rendering - /pre-rendering
Render pages to static HTML at build time (vike build) — no production server, deploy to any static host; best when content changes infrequently. Full, partial (opt-in/opt-out per page or group), or conditional; parameterized routes enumerate URLs in onBeforePrerenderStart; SPA pages emit an empty shell; SSG = SSR + prerender. noExtraDir, dist/client, doNotPrerender.

## prerender - /prerender
Build-time config toggling static generation and tuning the build (not runtime). Global options: partial (suppress unresolved-parameterized-route warnings), noExtraDir, parallel (concurrency), disableAutoRun, enable: null (change settings without enabling), keepDistServer; page-level value only toggles on/off. Feeds onBeforePrerenderStart.

## onBeforePrerenderStart - /onBeforePrerenderStart
Supply the concrete URLs for a parameterized page during static builds, optionally with their data. Build-time only (never dev or request-time); returns URL strings or { url, pageContext } pairs — providing pageContext skips +data/+onBeforeRender for that URL (bulk-fetch optimization); URLs may target other pages. Precedes onPrerenderStart; PrerenderContext.

## onPrerenderStart - /onPrerenderStart
Inspect or rewrite the entire set of pages slated for static generation in one place; i18n locale expansion; add/remove/mutate pageContexts. Build-time, global; receives and returns prerenderContext.pageContexts. Runs after per-page URL enumeration.

## ssr - /ssr
Per-page (or subtree, or global) toggle for server-side rendering. Disabling yields client-only pages that render and load entirely in the browser — SPA mode, admin panels; building block alongside pre-rendering and <ClientOnly>. The server-rendering switch.

## stream - /stream
Send HTML to the browser in chunks as produced rather than after the full page — progressive rendering, better TTFB. Requires ssr: true; 'web' or 'node' force a stream API; +data does NOT trigger streaming (awaits fully); bots receive a non-streamed response by default. react-streaming.

## renderPage - /renderPage
SSR integration point for a custom server (Express, Fastify, Hono, Cloudflare, Vercel) — the catch-all middleware mounted last. From vike/server; takes pageContextInit with urlOriginal plus optional headers/user, returns pageContext.httpResponse (body, statusCode, headers, pipe for streaming, earlyHints). Unneeded when fully prerendered; runtime-agnostic (Node/edge). createDevMiddleware.

## server - /server
+server file: attach a server framework (Hono, Express, Fastify, H3, Elysia) or opt into Vike's built-in lightweight server; access the HTTP request via pageContext.runtime.req. fetch-compatible handler; production settings (port, TLS, static files) via a prod export; server-file HMR without full reload; standalone builds (standaloner, srvx) drop node_modules in production. @vikejs/hono, @vikejs/express, vike/fetch; supersedes the deprecated photon extension.

## escapeInject - /escapeInject
Server-only tagged template (vike/server) for assembling the full HTML document inside onRenderHtml; auto-escapes interpolated values to prevent XSS. Companion dangerouslySkipEscape() bypasses escaping for already-sanitized markup (framework output); templates nest to compose fragments. Builds the document body — distinct from controlling injected asset tags.

## injectFilter - /injectFilter
Callback returned from onRenderHtml giving fine-grained control over which asset tags Vike injects and where — force image preloads, drop font preloads. Classified InjectFilterEntry list (assetType, isEntry) placed at HTML_BEGIN / HTML_END / false; production-only (no effect in dev due to lazy transpiling); beta. Governs injected tags — distinct from escapeInject which builds the body.

## csp - /csp
Content-Security-Policy with a per-request nonce on Vike's own <script> tags; avoid 'unsafe-inline'; PCI-DSS-style compliance. Generates pageContext.cspNonce and a default script-src header; custom nonce generation or hand-written policy via +headersResponse; beta. Lock down inline scripts.

## headersResponse - /headersResponse
Attach static or dynamic HTTP response headers — Set-Cookie, Cache-Control, custom fields. Value as Record, tuple array, Headers object, or a function of pageContext; a mutable pageContext.headersResponse Headers object editable in any hook; final set on pageContext.httpResponse.headers; beta. Response-side counterpart to reading incoming headers.

## headers - /headers
Read incoming request headers and cookies in hooks. Normalized pageContext.headers (Record<string,string>) and raw pageContext.headersOriginal (populated by forwarding req.headers at the renderPage call site); wrap with new Headers(...). Request-side — response headers handled separately.

## redirects - /redirects
Global map permanently rerouting old or external links to new paths; URL aliases. Named (@id) and glob (*) segments, internal or external targets; server-handled requests only — skipped during client-side navigation; 301/permanent. Chiefly for redirecting inbound third-party links to renamed URLs.

## base-url - /base-url
Serve the app under a sub-path or host assets on a separate origin (CDN). vite.config base changes page and asset URLs together; baseAssets changes only asset URLs independently; baseServer shifts the server URL root separately. import.meta.env.BASE_URL in code; guard renderPage middleware with pageContext.isBaseMissing.

## env - /env
How environment variables are defined and which reach the browser. .env / .env.development / .env.production / .env.local files; only the PUBLIC_ENV__ prefix is bundled client-side, the rest server-only; prefer statically-replaced import.meta.env over process.env (tree-shaking), but config files must use process.env. Secrets vs public values.

## i18n - /i18n
Locales, localized URLs, translations. onBeforeRoute extracts the locale (path prefix, query, or domain TLD), exposes pageContext.locale, and hands the router a locale-stripped urlLogical; onPrerenderStart duplicates pageContexts per locale for SSG; split locale data with +onBeforeRender.shared.js to avoid shipping every translation. modifyUrl, lang.

## modifyUrl - /modifyUrl
Pure helper rewriting parts of a URL string — pathname, protocol, hostname, port — returning the modified string; no routing side effects. Main use: stripping/rewriting locale prefixes for i18n. vike/modifyUrl; targeted URL surgery, not navigation.

## hooksTimeout - /hooksTimeout
Per-hook warning and error timeouts (milliseconds) for legitimately slow hooks. First logs a warning, then an error that renders the error page; a tier or the whole map can be set false, though disabling is strongly discouraged. Guards against hung hook promises.

## Page - /Page
Give a page its root UI component. Vike only exposes it at pageContext.Page; a UI-framework extension (vike-react/vue/solid) or your own onRenderHtml/onRenderClient actually renders it; exactly one per page. The minimal page building block.

## Layout - /Layout
Wrap pages with shared visual structure — header, sidebar, footer; nested layouts, tab/section shells. Cumulative, nests outer→inner around +Page, scoped to a directory subtree via config inheritance; appearance, not tool wiring. children/slot, keepScrollPosition; use +Wrapper for providers.

## Wrapper - /Wrapper
Enclose the entire page structure including all layouts — context providers, data-library roots, state/store boundaries. Sits outermost in the component tree so provider wrappers surround everything; multiple wrappers nest outward; cumulative. Integration, not visuals (vs +Layout).

## Head - /Head
Document-head tags that never change on navigation — favicon, Open Graph / crawler meta, PWA manifest, global scripts. Cumulative, injected into the first page's HTML only, server-emitted, not refreshed on client navigation — suits crawler-targeted/static tags but not a dynamic <title> (use +title). useConfig for dynamic head.

## client - /client
+client file: the very first browser JavaScript — error trackers, analytics, cookie consent, CSS-framework boot. Runs once at client startup before any rendering lifecycle, not re-run on navigation, currently single per app. Distinct from onHydrationEnd / onAfterRenderClient lifecycle hooks.

## ClientOnly - /ClientOnly
Render a component exclusively in the browser after hydration, with a fallback meanwhile — third-party widgets using window/document. From vike-react/vue/solid; tree-shakes wrapped children out of the server bundle so browser-only APIs are safe. Supersedes the older clientOnly() helper.

## useData - /useData
Read the +data result inside a UI component; runs both sides, scoped to the current page. Component-side alternative to pageContext.data, which isn't available inside components. From vike-react/vue/solid.

## usePageContext - /usePageContext
Read the full pageContext inside any component deep in the tree — urlPathname, routeParams, user. Broader than useData (all properties, not just data). From vike-react/vue/solid, or via React Context / Vue provide-inject.

## useHydrated - /useHydrated
Know inside a component whether hydration has completed — disable a control until interactive. Returns false on server and during the first client render, true afterward, triggering a re-render (unlike a static pageContext check). Guards interactivity needing a live DOM.

## getGlobalContext - /getGlobalContext
Reach the app-wide globalContext outside any page render — at server start before the first request; assets manifest, viteRuntimeInfo, isClientSide. Async, both sides; getGlobalContextAsync for early calls, getGlobalContextSync for production-only sync access. Also on pageContext.globalContext.

## data-fetching - /data-fetching
Overall strategy for getting data in/out of pages. Initial data via +data read with useData; mutations and later requests via RPC (Telefunc, tRPC) or thin API routes; app-wide data on globalContext/pageContext; SSG may need client-side +data. TanStack Query, vike-react-query, passToClient; decision guide tying the pieces together.

## auth - /auth
Authentication and page protection with any library (Better Auth, Auth.js, Passport.js, Auth0). Logged-in user as a custom pageContext.user set in +onCreatePageContext.server.js and exposed via passToClient; login flows and route protection via +guard with throw redirect or URL-preserving throw render; SSG apps init the user client-side via +onCreateGlobalContext.client.js. End-to-end auth pattern.

## store - /store
State management (Redux, Pinia, Zustand, Effector) under SSR without hydration mismatches. Identical initial state both sides: create store in +onCreatePageContext.ssr.js, capture in +onAfterRenderHtml, ship via passToClient, rehydrate in +onBeforeRenderClient; vike-react-redux/zustand, vike-vue-pinia automate it; +Wrapper, +onCreateApp. Central hazard: server/client state divergence.

## css-in-js - /css-in-js
Runtime CSS-in-JS under SSR without flash of unstyled content (FOUC); styled-components, styled-jsx, Grommet. Collect styles during render and inject into <head>: with extensions use +onBeforeRenderHtml/+onAfterRenderHtml plus useConfig; without, collect inside onRenderHtml. escapeInject, dangerouslySkipEscape; defining problem is SSR style collection.

## markdown - /markdown
Author pages in Markdown/MDX; blogs and docs sites; page metadata. @mdx-js/rollup, unplugin-vue-markdown; metadata scopes — global (available when rendering any page; a metadata module or eager custom setting) vs page-level (custom setting or frontmatter); avoid import.meta.glob over all +Page.md (lazy-transpiling conflict); MDX exports JS values usable as settings. globalContext.pages.

## graphql - /graphql
GraphQL clients with Vike — Apollo, Relay, urql. Manual integration follows the data-fetching pattern; extensions like vike-react-apollo; per-client example pages. GraphQL integration entry point.

## api-routes - /api-routes
REST-style HTTP endpoints — Vike has none built in by design. Define routes on your HTTP server (Express, Hono) and keep the renderPage middleware last so it doesn't intercept them; prefer RPC (Telefunc) for most data, reserve endpoints for file uploads, fine-grained HTTP caching, or a few simple cases.

## cli - /cli
Vike command-line tool — vike dev/build/preview/prerender — accepting any Vike setting as a flag. Flags or VIKE_CONFIG/VITE_CONFIG env vars in JSON5; --host, --port, --mode, --force. Vite's CLI works as an alternative but lacks Vike-specific flags.

## api - /api
Programmatic interface (vike/api) — dev(), build(), preview(), prerender() — to drive Vike from Node.js code; custom build pipelines, framework authoring, build post-processing. Each mirrors a command and accepts optional vikeConfig and viteConfig overrides; only serializable settings pass through vikeConfig. Scripting counterpart to the CLI.

## path-aliases - /path-aliases
Replace deep relative imports (../../../) with short prefixes (#root). Declare in vite.config resolve.alias for processed files and in tsconfig paths for TypeScript; for custom servers not using +server, also add to package.json imports so Node resolves them; config files use esbuild and read tsconfig/package.json, not vite.config.

## static-directory - /static-directory
Serve files as-is at their URL — robots.txt, plain assets, icons; Vite public/ folder. After build, served assets land in dist/client/ alongside content-hashed bundles; a Node server can serve dist/client/ directly via express.static to avoid copying heavy assets. Untouched static file serving.

## active-links - /active-links
Highlight the current page in navigation. Custom <Link> reading pageContext.urlPathname via usePageContext and applying an is-active class; the browser location object is unavailable during SSR. The SSR-safe active-navigation recipe.

## head-tags - /head-tags
Managing the document head — title, description, social image, viewport, arbitrary tags via +title/+description/+image/+viewport/+Head. Global or scoped; some replace (+title/+description/+image), some accumulate (+Head); dynamic values from data via useConfig; emitted even for non-SSR pages; manual path (head-manual) without UI extensions. Head-management hub.

## title - /title
Page title in the browser tab and og:title. Static string or a pageContext function; updates document.title during client-side navigation (unlike most head settings). Owns document.title and the social-title tag.

## description - /description
Page meta description and og:description for social sharing. Server-emitted, affects the initial HTML only, not later navigation. Owns the description metadata.

## image - /image
Social preview image when a URL is shared — og:image and twitter:card summary_large_image. An imported asset or a pageContext function. Owns the social-image metadata.

## viewport - /viewport
The mobile viewport meta tag. 'responsive' → width=device-width, initial-scale=1; a number fixes a virtual width that zooms desktop-only pages out on mobile; null omits the tag. Owns viewport behavior.

## htmlAttributes - /htmlAttributes
Merge attributes onto the <html> element — lang, a dark-mode class. Global, cumulative across files; a Record of attribute names to values. Owns outer-document-element attributes.

## bodyAttributes - /bodyAttributes
Merge attributes onto the <body> element — classes, theme flags. Global, cumulative; a Record of attribute names to values. Owns body-element attributes.

## SSR-vs-SPA - /SSR-vs-SPA
Per-page choice between server rendering and a browser-only app. SSR makes content visible to search and AI crawlers and improves first load; SPA is invisible to crawlers, needs no server, simpler — SSR for discoverable content, SPA for private/interaction-heavy screens. Expressed via the +ssr toggle; hydration, crawler visibility.

## common-issues - /common-issues
Typical breakages. Dependencies shipping invalid JavaScript that crash Node — workaround via vite.config ssr.noExternal; server/client output divergence — see the dedicated hydration-mismatch guide. First-stop troubleshooting.

## new - /new
Scaffold a fresh Vike project from the Bati generator around a chosen UI framework. Wires up the framework extension (vike-react/vue/solid) and options; separate path for an extension-free core setup. create-vike; create-a-new-app entry point.

## add - /add
Bring Vike into an existing Vite project incrementally. Swap Vite's CLI for Vike's, add a server or enable prerendering, define render hooks or adopt a framework extension, create a first +Page; choose SSR vs SSG, client vs server routing, with/without extensions. Progressive migration guide.

## integration - /integration
Hub for integrating any third-party tool not covered elsewhere — vanilla widgets via onHydrationEnd, analytics/service workers via +client, view transitions, component libraries, custom UI-framework rendering via onRenderHtml/onRenderClient, non-JavaScript backends (REST/GraphQL + JS SSR or SSG), manual server integration via renderPage middleware. Catch-all integration index.

## settings - /settings
Index of every built-in setting by category — basics (Page, Layout, Wrapper, route, prerender, ssr, stream, redirects), HTML shell (title, description, image, viewport, Head, lang, htmlAttributes, bodyAttributes, headHtml*, bodyHtml*), advanced (meta, passToClient, clientRouting, hydrationCanBeAborted, trailingSlash, injectScriptsAt). Custom settings via meta. Settings reference.

## deploy - /deploy
Deployment overview, provider-independent. Vike is just middleware and runs anywhere; prerendering can remove the production server entirely, shipping to a plain static host. Branches to specific targets.

## cloudflare - /cloudflare
Cloudflare Workers deployment; dev server runs in workerd (same runtime as production); static and SSR. @cloudflare/vite-plugin; bindings (D1, KV, Durable Objects) via the cloudflare:workers module; Durable Objects exported from +server; nodejs_compat opt-in; ~3MB worker bundle-size ceiling; wrangler. Workers deployment guide.

## cloudflare-pages - /cloudflare-pages
The older Cloudflare Pages product — static assets plus SSR via Pages Functions. Being phased out in favor of Workers; new projects should target those. Legacy Cloudflare path.

## vercel - /vercel
Vercel deployment, static or SSR. vite-plugin-vercel (recommended) with Incremental Static Regeneration (ISR) and per-route edge runtime; manual options — stable Vercel API Routes, more-flexible-but-less-stable Build Output API.

## netlify - /netlify
Fully prerendered, static Vike app on Netlify; for SSR use Netlify Functions instead. Build locally, in CI, or on the platform. Static-Netlify path.

## netlify-functions - /netlify-functions
Server-rendered Vike app on Netlify via serverless functions — the dynamic counterpart to the static-Netlify page; example repos. Netlify-functions path.

## github-pages - /github-pages
Publish a fully prerendered app to GitHub Pages. Needs a .nojekyll marker so underscore-prefixed filenames serve; typically deploys via a GitHub Actions workflow; base path set to /repo-name/ on a user subdomain. GitHub Pages guide.

## static-hosts - /static-hosts
Hosting a prerendered app on any static provider; build-and-upload strategy. Every page including SPA pages must be prerendered; local vike preview; base path configurable for sub-path hosting. Generic static-hosting guide.

## edgeone-pages - /edgeone-pages
Tencent EdgeOne Pages with SSR and HTML streaming. The @edgeone/vite adapter configured with serverWrapper: 'vike'; deploy by Git connection or the EdgeOne CLI.

## aws - /aws
AWS deployment including Lambda with any tool — Vike is framework-agnostic middleware. Full-stack: SST, Serverless Framework, Flightcontrol, Argonaut; Lambda-focused: Claudia.js, Apex Up, SAM; worked examples (Serverless Framework, SST + Hono).

## docker - /docker
Containerize Vike as an ordinary Node server; Docker/Podman quirks. HMR not working over websockets (WSL config); app unreachable from host until server.host is truthy (Vike auto-sets it when a container is detected); out-of-memory build failures fixed by raising --max-old-space-size.

## photon - /photon
Deprecated, replaced by the +server file. Formerly the vike-photon extension configuring server bundling and standalone output. Use the current server integration instead.
